title: Jijamata Udyaan
tagline: Jijamata Udyaan — wildlife reserve of Maharashtra

overview.short: Jijamata Udyaan, also known as Victoria Gardens and the Byculla Zoo, is a zoo and garden covering 50 acres located at Byculla, in the heart of Mumbai, India.
overview.description: Jijamata Udyaan, also known as Victoria Gardens and the Byculla Zoo, is a zoo and garden covering 50 acres located at Byculla, in the heart of Mumbai, India. It is the oldest public garden in Mumbai. After Indian independence, it was officially renamed after Jijamata, the mother of Shivaji, the first Maratha emperor; the original name remains popular. In 1835, the British administration granted a large plot of land in Sewri to the Agro Horticultural Society of Western India for a botanical garden known as Victoria Gardens, after Queen-Empress Victoria.

nearestAirport.name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Byculla
topPlaces.description: Byculla (ISO: Bhāykhaḷā; pronunciation: [bʱaːjkʰəɭaː]) is an area of South Mumbai.

topPlaces.name: Jewish Cemetery, Chinchpokli
topPlaces.description: Jewish Cemetery, Chinchpokli, is a cemetery in Chinchpokli, Bombay, laid out near the Chinchpokli railway station by Elias David Sassoon in 1878. Covering two acres, the burial ground now contains more than a thousand graves, and new burials continue to take place.

topPlaces.name: Aga Khan's Maqbara
topPlaces.description: The Aga Khan's Maqbara or Aga Khan's Dargah is the mausoleum of Aga Khan I, located in Mazgaon, Mumbai, India. It is also known as the Hasanabad Monument since the personal name of Aga Khan I was Hasan.

topPlaces.name: Chinchpokli
topPlaces.description: Chinchpokli (Marathi: चिंचपोकळी) is a neighbourhood in South Mumbai. It is also the name of a railway station on the Central line of the Mumbai suburban railway.

topPlaces.name: Dagdi Chawl
topPlaces.description: Dagdi Chawl is a neighbourhood in Byculla, Mumbai, used to house mill workers from the nearby industries. Now it is known for the fortified home of mafia don turned politician Arun Gawli.

topPlaces.name: Khada Parsi
topPlaces.description: Khada Parsi, Ubha Parsi (transl. Standing Parsi) or Cursetjee Fountain is a Grade 1 heritage monument located in Byculla, Mumbai, India. Installed in 1867, the monument features a life-sized cast iron statue of Cursetjee Manockjee (19 August 1763 — 7 May 1845), a prominent Parsi businessman and philanthropist, atop a pillar and surrounded by mermaid fountains.

topPlaces.name: Ghodapdeo
topPlaces.description: Ghodapdeo is a neighborhood in Byculla, Mumbai. Ghodapdeo lies between Cotton Green, Reay Road, Raani Baug, Mustafa Bazaar and Kaala Chowki.

topPlaces.name: Agripada
topPlaces.description: Agripada is an area in South Mumbai. It is located between Byculla (West) and Mumbai Central (East).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jijamata Udyaan?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jijamata Udyaan is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jijamata Udyaan?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (13 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jijamata Udyaan?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jijamata Udyaan at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jijamata Udyaan?
faq.a: Stays in Jijamata Udyaan start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jijamata Udyaan?
faq.a: The top attractions are Byculla, Jewish Cemetery, Chinchpokli, Aga Khan's Maqbara, Chinchpokli, Dagdi Chawl.
